title: quercus coccifera subsp. mesto
intro: Quercus coccifera subsp. mesto, also known as the holm oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree or shrub in the beech family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The bark and leaves of the Quercus coccifera subsp. mesto have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and wounds, due to its high tannin content and other bioactive compounds. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
